content:
```json
{
  "mnemonics": ["Numpy's Heart Beats with ND-Arrays. Think of it as the Numerical Data (ND) core that pumps data through the entire SciPy ecosystem."],
  "keyPoints": ["The ndarray is the central data structure of NumPy, providing efficient storage and operations for homogeneous, n-dimensional data.", "It enables vectorized operations, which eliminate the need for explicit looping and are highly optimized for performance.", "Its attributes like shape, dtype, and strides define the structure, data type, and memory layout of the data, respectively."],
  "commonMistakes": ["Assuming that operations on ndarrays will modify the data in-place; many operations return a new array instead.", ["Confusing the concepts of a view (a new array object that looks at the same data) and a copy (a new array with its own data).", "Attempting to perform operations on arrays with incompatible shapes, leading to broadcasting errors or unintended results."]
}
```
